name: Aromatic Duck
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the wingtips from the duck and wrap them with the giblets for making stock.
2. Pull out and discard any loose interior fat.
3. Rinse the duck inside and out, then pat dry thoroughly.
4. In a spice mill or mortar, mix together the peppercorns, thyme, bay leaves, cloves, and salt. Grind to a fine texture.
5. Combine in a small dish with the brown sugar and brandy.
6. Place the duck in a heavy plastic bag that fits snugly.
7. Rub half the spice mixture inside the duck and the remaining half over the outside.
8. Place the garlic in the duck's cavity.
9. Close the bag tightly and refrigerate for 24 hours, occasionally rubbing the surface and squeezing in the spices.
10. Remove the duck from the bag and gently pat it dry. Set on a cake rack over a plate and return to the refrigerator for another 24 hours or so, turning once.
11. Pat the duck thoroughly until dry. Let come to room temperature for at least two hours.
12. Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
13. Using a large needle and white cotton thread, sew closed the neck and tail openings.
14. Place the duck on its side on a rack set in a roasting pan.
15. Roast the duck for 45 minutes.
16. Prick the fatty parts on the upper side with the needle; do not prick deeply enough to pierce the flesh.
17. Turn the duck onto the other side and increase oven temperature to 350°F (175°C). Roast for another 30 minutes.
18. Prick the fatty parts on the upside again, then turn the duck onto its breast.
19. Increase heat to 400°F (200°C) and roast about 30 minutes longer, or until deeply browned and most of the fat has melted away.
20. Remove from oven and let cool for 1 to 3 hours before serving.
21. To serve: Using a heavy cleaver or poultry shears, cut through the flesh and bone carefully to make about 12 to 14 servings, keeping the skin intact on each piece.
22. Arrange the pieces over cabbage leaves on a platter, shallow basket, or wooden bowl.
23. If serving hot, arrange the cut pieces in a single layer with the cut side down in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15 minutes.
